steps.label: cause
steps.text: Different yeast species and strains ferment the same must under otherwise comparable conditions.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Their metabolism produces different proportions of esters, higher alcohols, sulfur compounds and other volatile molecules.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Yeast choice can materially influence the wine's fermentation aroma profile and stylistic expression.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: yeast strain affects aroma because strains generate different volatile metabolites during fermentation.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
